titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Alfred Whitney Griswold, President of Yale University, Records
Griswold (Alfred Whitney), President of Yale University, Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Manuscripts and Archives staff

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday May 7, 2021 at 09:19

did.unittitle: Alfred Whitney Griswold, president of Yale University, records
did.abstract: The records consist of correspondence, subject files, reports, planning memoranda, and miscellaneous biographical material documenting A. Whitney Griswold's activities as president of Yale University. Included are materials concerning alumni relations; academic freedom and Communism; funds, gifts, trusts, and other development activities; Yale committees; athletics and the Ivy League; admissions; Yale commencements; relations with the city of New Haven; the Association of American Universities; the publication of William F. Buckley, Jr.'s book, God and Man at Yale ; the Masters of Arts in Teaching program; and the National Defense Education Act. Also included are a set of Griswold"s addresses, articles, and other writings; and some personal materials.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 2-A (YRG 2-A), Records of the Yale president"s office.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the records is partially restricted. See Collection Contents for details.
Some records in this finding aid have been redacted, as they include student names, donor names, and other restricted data. These records will not appear in the published finding aid.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Alfred Whitney Griswold, President of Yale University, Records (RU 22). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Yale University records are arranged and described at the accession level by the creating office. The University Archives creates collection level descriptive records, but typically does no further arrangement and description at the accession level.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Alfred Whitney Griswold was born on October 27, 1906 in Morristown, New Jersey. He received a B.A. from Yale in 1929 and a Ph.D. in 1933. Griswold taught English and was a professor of history, as well as government and international relations, at Yale. He served as Director of the U.S. Army Civil Affairs Training Program in Foreign Area and Language Studies, and as Director of the U.S. Army Civil Affairs Training School at Yale (1942). In 1950 Griswold became president of Yale. He instituted progressive academic and physical changes and introduced discussion on coeducation at Yale. A. Whitney Griswold died in New Haven, Connecticut on April 19, 1963.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of correspondence, subject files, reports, planning memoranda, and miscellaneous biographical material documenting A. Whitney Griswold"s activities as president of Yale University. Included are materials concerning alumni relations; academic freedom and Communism; funds, gifts, trusts, and other development activities; Yale committees; athletics and the Ivy League; admissions; Yale commencements; relations with the city of New Haven; the Association of American Universities; the publication of William F. Buckley, Jr.'s book, God and Man at Yale ; the Masters of Arts in Teaching program; and the National Defense Education Act. Also included are a set of Griswold's addresses, articles, and other writings; and some personal materials.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged as follows: I. Subject/Correspondence Files. II. Writings. III. Miscellany; subsequent accessions.

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

New Yorker Article re Yale Admissions Policies
"We Must be Peacemakers in a Free World" New York Times Magazine Article by Chas. Poore re President-Elect Griswold
Saturday Review Article: "More Money for Our Colleges"
Time Magazine re Griswold ("51); re Saarinen ("56)
The Times of London Literary Supplement Article: "The Proper Study of Mankind"
The Times of London Educational Supplements
United States Dept. of Health, Ed. and Welfare Pamphlet: Engineering Enrollments and Degrees
University of the State of New York Pamphlet: "The Schools We Need"
University of the State of New York, Allen, James E. (Addresses and Papers)
Addresses and Speeches from Oct 6, 1950 [various copies]
Aid to Colleges for Education Costs, Gifts, Scholarships
News Clips re Coeducation at Yale
Expansion, General College, in the U.S.
Faculty Promotions and Chairs and Fellowships
Pres. Griswold - Misc. Events or Appearances
Master of Arts in Teaching Program

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Re School Sites in New Haven
Additional Information on Speeches and Various Engagements
Speech by A.W.G. on Program for Arts and Sciences
Speeches, Articles, and Reports of A.W.G.
List of AWG's Speaking Engagements, Out-of-Town Meetings, etc.
Additional copies of AWG's books: In the University Tradition , Liberal Education and the Democratic Ideal (2 copies), Essays on Education
AWG Funeral: Seating Assignments, Persons Notified
Letters of Sympathy received by Mr. R. Holden
Newspaper Articles on Death of AWG

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The accession consists of a scrapbook documenting the presidential election and inauguration of Alfred Whitney Griswold.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
